summary: No-ship: the RED witness is valid and P-1/P-2 are repaired, but P-3 remains unsealed. No new critical issue found. Simpler alternative: retain source-absence classification and rename/fsync separation, but enforce each call site directly instead of relying on the wrapper’s incomplete type barrier.

findings.title: P-3 acceptance can pass while recover_graves remains broken
findings.body: The plan claims W2 kills reverting `recover_graves` to raw `?`, but W2 only exercises `Entry::rename_durable_to` directly. An implementation can reconstruct the grave path from `entry.name()` and `Layout`, then retain the existing `atomic::rename_durable(...).await?`. This introduces none of W8's prohibited strings, while existing tests do not make a grave disappear between snapshot and rename. All stated acceptance checks can therefore pass even though a vanished grave still aborts the entire reconciliation pass.
findings.recommendation: Add a call-site witness that removes a snapshotted grave before recovery and asserts the pass continues. Also make W8 reject direct `atomic::rename_durable` calls from `reconcile.rs`, or otherwise structurally prevent bypassing `Entry::rename_durable_to`.

next_steps: Revise the P-3 acceptance evidence to cover actual recover_graves routing, then rerun the plan gate.
Keep the existing P-1 dangling-symlink and P-2 post-rename fsync design unchanged.
